behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm’s quality bound, which can be exercised in-process with deterministic coordinate fixtures and known optimal baselines. MOCK-MISS: this behavior is about algorithm output quality, not cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require interaction failures between components; it only asserts the heuristic ratio property.
behaviors.test_description: Run table-driven tests on fixed stop sets (sizes up to 20) with precomputed optimal lengths; assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is formula/config mapping correctness (time from leg distance and road-type speed), which is testable as pure computation with deterministic inputs. MOCK-MISS: no required multi-module boundary is stated. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real infrastructure dependency. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on computation correctness, not orchestration between external components.
behaviors.test_description: Call the estimator with explicit leg distances and road types plus speed config; assert per-leg and aggregated window outputs, including rounding/units edge cases.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: This is a local validation rule (compare total time vs remaining shift and compute excess). MOCK-MISS: no module interaction required by behavior text. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejected Integration because no handoff boundary is needed to expose this arithmetic/validation failure.
behaviors.test_description: Test validator with boundary values (equal, just over, far over) and assert reject/accept status plus exact excess duration.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff: geocoding result filtering -> optimizer input -> skipped-address reporting. A single-function unit test would miss wiring/state propagation defects. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required because a deterministic in-process geocoder stub can return ‘no results’; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. Rejected System because behavior text only requires handling an API ‘no results’ response, not real network behavior/timeouts/protocol effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with a fake geocoder adapter that returns coordinates for N-1 addresses and no results for one; assert optimized route excludes failed stop and response includes that address in skipped list.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is preprocessing + route construction interaction: dedupe/merge logic must propagate into optimizer inputs and final route cardinality. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only merge helper can miss duplication reintroduced downstream. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no AI/ML dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run service flow in-process with duplicate addresses carrying different item lists; assert one consolidated stop exists, items are merged correctly, and route visits that location once.
